questions.q: On dissolution of a partnership firm, the assets are realized at:
What is the accounting treatment of goodwill on dissolution of a partnership firm?
questions.answer: Goodwill is treated as an asset and is realized at its book value and is adjusted in the capital accounts of partners.

questions.q: The effect of revaluation of assets and liabilities on capital accounts of partners at the time of dissolution of a firm is to be shown in the:
What is the accounting treatment given to the partners' capital and reserve accounts when the firm is dissolved?
questions.answer: When the firm is dissolved, the partners' capital and reserve accounts are closed by transferring their balances to the partners' capital accounts in their profit-sharing ratio. The amount of each partner's capital and reserve accounts is ascertained by preparing a balance sheet of the firm at the time of dissolution.

questions.q: Dissolution of a partnership firm is:
The profit or loss on revaluation of assets and liabilities is credited or debited to:
What is the accounting treatment of the firm's liabilities on dissolution of the partnership firm?
questions.answer: The firm's liabilities are paid off in the order of their priority. The amounts of liabilities are deducted from the assets of the firm to arrive at the amount available for distribution among the partners.

questions.q: The effect of dissolution of a partnership firm on the personal capital of partners is that their:
questions.answer: Personal Capitals are affected as the firm's assets are distributed among the partners in proportion to their capital and profit-sharing ratio.

questions.q: Dissolution of a partnership firm results in:
What is the accounting treatment of the firm's assets on dissolution of the partnership firm?
questions.answer: The firm's assets are realized at their book value and the amounts received are distributed among the partners in their profit-sharing ratio.
